| [.claude](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/.claude ".claude") | [.claude](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/.claude ".claude") | [Working version.](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/4033322f9b23e0786ee1abd6ec19205c125b9132 "Working version.") | Feb 20, 2026 |
| [Sources](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/Sources "Sources") | [Sources](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/Sources "Sources") | [Touch: hard-exit on describe-all exhaustion; preflight invokes venv b…](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/dcce287cca1b787378bf96e490a9b341166516f2 "Touch: hard-exit on describe-all exhaustion; preflight invokes venv binaries Two gaps allowed a stale-shebang venv (e.g. after renaming the repo) to slip past startup checks: start.sh only tested the executable bit on .venv/bin/{python,idb} so a broken interpreter path was invisible, and TouchInjector silently called markNotReady when describe-all retries ran out, leaving the HTTP server serving video with dead touches. - start.sh now actually invokes the venv interpreter and idb wrapper. - TouchInjector prints the diagnostic box and exit(7) when retries exhaust on a booted UDID. The no-booted-simulator path stays soft so booting after start still recovers. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | May 23, 2026 |
| [browser](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/browser "browser") | [browser](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/browser "browser") | [Full reconnect when tab returns from background](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/011b61cb2b0caa8d4d8406a19728656646953e8c "Full reconnect when tab returns from background Soft 'resume' was leaving the stream stale on visibilitychange — the user had to click the in-app refresh arrow to recover. Match that behavior automatically by calling reconnect() (full ws + decoder teardown, then fresh connect) when the tab becomes visible again. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | May 23, 2026 |
| [skills/stream-simulator](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/skills/stream-simulator "This path skips through empty directories") | [skills/stream-simulator](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/tree/main/skills/stream-simulator "This path skips through empty directories") | [Default to cloudflared tunnel so an off-device agent can view the stream](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/591711c1a55b700041315365f1e8a7e9b12e2541 "Default to cloudflared tunnel so an off-device agent can view the stream The original UX inverted the most-common use case: SimStream's primary purpose is to show the running Simulator to an off-device viewer (Claude, a phone over the open internet, etc.), but that required passing --tunnel every time. Local-only viewing is the minority case. - start.sh: tunnel mode is now default. Pass --no-tunnel to skip cloudflared for a local-only run. Updated help text and error messages accordingly. - SKILL.md: instruct the agent to check `command -v cloudflared` before launch — if present, run the default; if absent, fall back to --no-tunnel and tell the user that `brew install cloudflared` is quick if they want to share the stream remotely. - README.md: examples and dependency list updated to match. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | May 23, 2026 |
| [.gitignore](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/.gitignore ".gitignore") | [.gitignore](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/.gitignore ".gitignore") | [Consolidate signaling server into the Swift process](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/26f3c386a27f163bf9e0adfb1a9164ac917a346a "Consolidate signaling server into the Swift process The Node relay had become a dumb proxy once WebRTC was out (no SDP/ICE signaling anymore — just binary-frame forwarding and static HTML). Rolling it into the Swift app drops the Node dependency and makes the system run as one process. - HTTPServer.swift: NWListener-backed HTTP server that serves index.html on GET / and handles the WebSocket upgrade handshake (SHA-1 accept via CryptoKit) on GET /ws, all on a single port so Cloudflare quick tunnels still work (they forward only one URL). - WebSocketConnection.swift: minimal RFC 6455 framing on NWConnection — send text/binary, read masked client frames, reply to pings. - StreamingApp owns the HTTPServer directly; single-viewer policy (a new connection evicts the previous one). WS open = viewerJoined + idle pump, WS close = viewerLeft. - Dropped the register / streamer-ready handshake from the browser — the WS connection itself is now the registration signal. - start.sh launches just the Swift app (plus optional cloudflared). - Default port bumped to 3738 (rare enough to avoid common dev-server collisions); start.sh probes 3738..3742 and picks the first free one. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | Apr 19, 2026 |
| [Package.swift](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/Package.swift "Package.swift") | [Package.swift](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/Package.swift "Package.swift") | [Replace WebRTC with WebCodecs + WebSocket](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/2949145a27f9bf3d6a4be0cec0e84c119a7770e5 "Replace WebRTC with WebCodecs + WebSocket WebRTC needed a TURN relay to traverse restrictive home NATs, and Cloudflare tunnels (TCP-only) can't carry WebRTC traffic anyway. Running our own TURN server or paying for one adds cost and ops; using WebCodecs over a WebSocket keeps everything on the existing HTTPS/WS path that already traverses any tunnel or reverse proxy. Pipeline now: SCStream CVPixelBuffer → VTCompressionSession (H.264 Baseline, low-latency rate control, MaxFrameDelayCount=0, MTU-sized slices) → Annex-B over WebSocket → browser VideoDecoder (prefer-hardware, optimizeForLatency) → canvas.drawImage. Binary frame format: [1 byte key flag][8 bytes pts µs BE][Annex-B]. Keyframes inline SPS+PPS so a fresh viewer can latch on without any separate description negotiation. One JSON video-init announces the avc1 codec string + dimensions before the first keyframe. Capture bumped to 60fps to halve sampling latency. Browser requests a keyframe on visibilitychange → visible for iOS Safari tab resume. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | Apr 19, 2026 |
| [README.md](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [README.md](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [Add demo video](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/e42485cf8f277761a4c047be836894b363bf51a1 "Add demo video") | May 23, 2026 |
| [demo.mp4](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/demo.mp4 "demo.mp4") | [demo.mp4](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/demo.mp4 "demo.mp4") | [Add demo video to README](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/1f3443ff53245ea274363b95ae9b3a08202f58d1 "Add demo video to README Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | May 23, 2026 |
| [idb_touch_events_bridge.py](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/idb_touch_events_bridge.py "idb_touch_events_bridge.py") | [idb_touch_events_bridge.py](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/idb_touch_events_bridge.py "idb_touch_events_bridge.py") | [Hard-fail on missing deps, permissions, and touch-pipeline errors](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/a0e3369e7892577179e2448fb3a90738679e19b4 "Hard-fail on missing deps, permissions, and touch-pipeline errors - Rename install.sh → install_idb.sh; support both uv and pip; install idb-companion via brew alongside the venv. - start.sh preflight-checks swift, idb_companion, .venv/python, .venv/idb, bridge script, and (with --tunnel) cloudflared, aborting with the fix for each missing piece. - Permission preflight at app startup: exit on missing Screen Recording or Accessibility, both with troubleshooting steps. - HTTP server and ScreenCaptureKit failures now exit with explained causes instead of leaving the RunLoop spinning silently. - TouchInjector: readiness state, loud one-shot errors with troubleshooting on every failure path, SIGPIPE-safe writes, bridge-process termination handler, auto-retry resolve on next touch. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | May 23, 2026 |
| [install_idb.sh](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/install_idb.sh "install_idb.sh") | [install_idb.sh](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/blob/main/install_idb.sh "install_idb.sh") | [Hard-fail on missing deps, permissions, and touch-pipeline errors](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream/commit/a0e3369e7892577179e2448fb3a90738679e19b4 "Hard-fail on missing deps, permissions, and touch-pipeline errors - Rename install.sh → install_idb.sh; support both uv and pip; install idb-companion via brew alongside the venv. - start.sh preflight-checks swift, idb_companion, .venv/python, .venv/idb, bridge script, and (with --tunnel) cloudflared, aborting with the fix for each missing piece. - Permission preflight at app startup: exit on missing Screen Recording or Accessibility, both with troubleshooting steps. - HTTP server and ScreenCaptureKit failures now exit with explained causes instead of leaving the RunLoop spinning silently. - TouchInjector: readiness state, loud one-shot errors with troubleshooting on every failure path, SIGPIPE-safe writes, bridge-process termination handler, auto-retry resolve on next touch. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>") | May 23, 2026 |

## Repository files navigation

*   [README](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#)

# About SimStream

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#about-simstream)

Use the iOS Simulator screen on a browser (mobile or not). Low-latency, limited dependencies.

## Demo video

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#demo-video)

No.wifi_compressed.mp4[Video 3](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/9083711/597152493-98e72b20-35de-4daa-b010-dc3c413a110d.mp4?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..DQ2wM9vlWVfsHWzES7kHOzHx9v1AHJn16lgeOGUCn_Q)

## Dependencies

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#dependencies)

*   Swift/Xcode
*   `idb_companion` (`brew install facebook/fb/idb-companion`): send touch events to the simulator
*   Python package manager (pip or uv) to run the idb Python bridge ()
*   `cloudflared` (`brew install cloudflared`): needed to expose the stream to an agent like Claude. Skip with `--no-tunnel` if you only want a local URL.
*   For streaming: Chrome 94+ or Safari 16.4+ (needs `VideoDecoder`)

## Permissions

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#permissions)

Grant both to Terminal (System Settings → Privacy & Security):

*   **Screen Recording** — required to capture the Simulator window via ScreenCaptureKit.
*   **Accessibility** — used to locate the device-screen rect inside the Simulator window so the macOS title bar is cropped out and touches map to the right device coordinates.

When running inside a MacOS VM, you can force-enable those permissions by modifying TCC.db (integrity protection needs to be disabled, it's the case on Tart VMs)

## Installation (with agent skill)

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#installation-with-agent-skill)

undefinedshell
npx skills add EliotAndres/SimStream
undefined

Installs a `/stream-simulator` skill into your coding agent. From then on, ask the agent to start SimStream (or paste an error) and it will handle setup, launch, permission prompts, and troubleshooting for you.

## Installation (manual)

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#installation-manual)

undefinedshell
./install_idb.sh
undefined

Installs `idb-companion` via Homebrew and creates a Python venv for the touch bridge. Uses `uv` if present, otherwise falls back to `python3 -m venv` + `pip`.

## Run

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#run)

undefinedshell
./start.sh              # default: serves http://localhost:3738 AND a Cloudflare tunnel
                        # URL so an agent (Claude, etc.) can open the stream remotely.
./start.sh --no-tunnel  # local only — skip cloudflared. Use when you're viewing
                        # from a browser on the same machine or LAN.
undefined

## Notes (AI Generated)

[](https://github.com/EliotAndres/SimStream#notes-ai-generated)

*   The Swift process disables `Window → Show Device Bezels` on launch (cleaner capture).
*   A single Swift binary handles capture, H.264 encoding (hardware with software fallback), HTTP, and WebSocket — no separate signaling server.
*   Touch events are forwarded to `idb_companion` over a persistent gRPC bridge (`idb_touch_events_bridge.py`).
*   Override the port with `PORT=xxxx swift run SimulatorStream`.
*   **macOS VMs / Tart guests:** VideoToolbox often creates an H.264 session but never delivers frames (`kVTVideoEncoderNotAvailableNowErr`). The encoder falls back to software H.264 after a few failed output callbacks. To skip hardware from the first frame, set `SIMULATOR_STREAM_PREFER_SOFTWARE_ENCODER=1` (e.g. `SIMULATOR_STREAM_PREFER_SOFTWARE_ENCODER=1 ./start.sh`).

## About

SimStream is a library to stream iOS simulators from your Mac to your iPhone (or any web browser)
